documentation.value: A JSON object containing key-value pairs. Keys must be double-quoted strings.
A double-quoted string serving as the property name.
A valid JSON value: string, number, boolean, null, object, or array.
A JSON array containing an ordered list of values.
Any valid JSON value: string, number, boolean, null, object, or array.
A JSON string value enclosed in double quotes. Supports escape sequences: \n, \t, \\, \", \uXXXX.
A JSON number. Can be integer or floating point, with optional exponent (e.g., 42, 3.14, 1.5e10). No leading zeros, NaN, or Infinity.
JSON boolean literals. Must be lowercase.
Represents an empty or non-existent value in JSON.
Specifies a JSON Schema URL for validation and editor autocompletion support.
The URL of the JSON Schema to validate against.
A JSON Reference pointer used in JSON Schema to reference a definition.
A JSON Pointer path to the referenced definition.
